cards.front: What happens when a subclass provides a different implementation of a method that is already defined in its superclass?
How do Java programs determine which method to call when there are multiple methods with the same name in a class hierarchy?
What is the purpose of an abstract class in Java?
cards.back: An abstract class is a class that cannot be instantiated on its own and is designed to be subclassed by other classes.

cards.front: What happens when an abstract class has a final method?
cards.back: The subclass cannot override the final method and must provide an implementation of its own.

cards.front: What is the benefit of declaring a local variable without specifying its type in Java?
cards.back: Local Variable Type Inference (LVTI) allows the compiler to automatically infer the type of the variable based on its initializer.
